title: Madhesh Province - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Madhesh Province Birgunj launches drive to demolish over 1,000 roadside structures By Shankar Acharya Local residents and traders protest sudden action as authorities enforce 25-metre road standard along key highway stretch. Birgunj begins demolition drive on encroached structures By Shankar Acharya Around 1,200 private buildings and 29 government structures fall within a 25-metre road widening pl
content:
Madhesh Province Birgunj launches drive to demolish over 1,000 roadside structures By Shankar Acharya Local residents and traders protest sudden action as authorities enforce 25-metre road standard along key highway stretch. Birgunj begins demolition drive on encroached structures By Shankar Acharya Around 1,200 private buildings and 29 government structures fall within a 25-metre road widening plan from Gandak Chowk to Miteri Bridge. Madhesh crackdown on Indian-registered vehicles rattles political parties By Shiva Puri Major political parties have opposed the move and demanded free movement within 30 kilometres of the Nepal-India border. Loan sharking rampant in Rautahat, over 7,000 victims file complaints against moneylenders By Shiva Puri Victims recount tales of financial ruin and legal harassment at the hands of a local trader, as they struggle for justice despite government intervention. RSP’s Amarkanta Chaudhary defeats Upendra Yadav in Saptari-3 By Post Report Chaudhary secures 32,875 votes, beating the JSP-N chair by over 17,000 votes. RSP’s Shambhu Yadav wins Siraha-3 seat By Binay Aazad Yadav defeats nearest rival Lilanath Shrestha of UML by over 22,000 votes. Ramakanta Prasad Chaurasiya wins Parsa-3 By Post Report The RSP candidate defeats Nepali Congress’ Surendra Prasad Chaudhary by 13,313 votes. RSP’s Amresh Singh leads by 3,209 votes in Sarlahi-4 By Post Report Rastriya Swatantra Party maintains commanding advantage over Congress president Gagan Kumar Thapa. RSP’s Rajesh Chaudhary leads in Rautahat-1, former PM Madhav Kumar Nepal trails By Post Report Early counting shows Rastriya Swatantra Party ahead as Nepal ranks fifth in his former stronghold. Politician-turned-journalist Rishi Dhamala trails heavily in Rautahat-4 By Shiva Puri Out of 5,092 votes counted, Dhamala has secured only 10 votes. Police arrest five including JSP-N candidate Yadav By Binay Aazad Siraha–3 parliamentary candidate held with Rs487,000 in unaccounted cash. Nepali Army disposes of suspicious object in Rautahat By Shiva Puri Locals panic after object found near school; army says bomb disposal teams are deployed across constituencies. Local clash in Mahottari, police fire tear gas shells By Santosh Singh Minor incident escalates into property damage, police intervene to restore order. Campaign launched to restore Birgunj’s BP Park By Shankar Acharya The park, spread over 6.7 hectares near Ghantaghar, had been taken over by shacks and waste. Curfew lifted in Gaur after peace march By Shiva Puri Local leaders, residents march through Sabgadh to show unity. Land compensation delays stall Bardi­bas railway project By Ajit Tiwari Unpaid compensation for 14 households halts construction on Bhangaha–Bardi­bas rail stretch, residents hopeful as ministry review continues. Curfew continues in Gaur after Saturday clashes By Shiva Puri Municipality remains tense as authorities maintain restrictions following stone-pelting and vehicle burning. Two dead in Rautahat road accident By Post Report Motorbike crashes into tree after losing control; both riders die in hospital. Siraha prison inmates continue protest after arms, cash seizure By Binay Aazad Authorities seize pistol, ammunition, cash during monitoring; inmates resort to arson and vandalism. Locals block Hulaki Highway after car plunges into river in Siraha By Binay Aazad Three injured in midnight crash; residents demand action against contractor and immediate bridge construction.
